body.title: Librarian owns the compiled-inference proxy layer
body.description:
The Librarian becomes the owner of the inference proxy. Every inference call already flows through the proxy; the Librarian adds a graph-tree pre-check layer before the call reaches any LLM. On a hit the answer returns locally (zero upstream cost). On a miss the call goes to the LLM and the proxy fans out: answer back to caller, AND (request + response + log pointer) enqueued for Librarian overnight learning. Callers never change — escalation routing and compilation happen invisibly in the proxy.

Whether the Librarian IS the proxy (merged) or OWNS it (wraps it) is a TBD implementation detail to resolve in sprint.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/librarian/ (extend); devices/inference/ (proxy integration); TBD on merged vs. owned shape

**Design rules:** bus+shim model; no-sqlite; log every interface crossing (AR-009); compiled inference — graph-tree pre-check fires before any LLM call

**Scope boundary:** IN — proxy ownership by Librarian; graph-tree pre-check stub (always-miss initially is fine); dual output fan-out (answer + learning queue). OUT — overnight learning pipeline (T-inference-learning-pipeline); chat log bootstrap (T-chat-log-learning-bootstrap); emotional encoding distinction (handled in learning pipeline)

**Completion criteria:** (1) every inference call routed through Librarian-owned proxy; (2) graph-tree pre-check runs before LLM call (stub that always misses is valid at ship); (3) on LLM response, learning payload enqueued to Librarian; (4) caller API unchanged; (5) PROXY_INTERCEPT logged at INFO for every call
